cases.description: User wants periodic codebase health pass with no specific target
cases.prompt: Find refactoring opportunities in this codebase.
expectations: Skill architecture-review is selected
Step 1: agent reads docs/CONTEXT.md and relevant docs/adr/* before exploring
Step 1: agent dispatches Task subagent (subagent_type=Explore) for the codebase walk
Step 2: agent presents a NUMBERED list of deepening candidates
Each candidate uses CONTEXT.md vocabulary for domain terms (not 'service', 'component', 'API', 'boundary')
Each candidate uses architecture vocabulary (module, interface, depth, seam, adapter)
Agent does NOT propose interfaces yet — asks 'which to explore?'

cases.description: User picks candidate 3; agent runs Step 3 grilling loop
cases.prompt: Let's explore candidate 3 — the Order intake module.
expectations: Step 3 grilling loop begins
Agent classifies dependencies (in-process / local-substitutable / remote-but-owned / true-external)
Agent picks a test strategy based on the classification
If a new term enters the conversation, agent updates docs/CONTEXT.md inline
If the user rejects the candidate with a load-bearing reason, agent offers to record an ADR (triple test applies)
Agent does NOT execute the refactor — that is refactor-workflow's job

cases.description: Agent must apply deletion test to candidate shallow modules
cases.prompt: I think the OrderValidator module is too thin.
expectations: Agent applies the deletion test: imagine deleting OrderValidator
If complexity vanishes, agent confirms it is a pass-through (deepening candidate)
If complexity reappears across N callers, agent reports the module was earning its keep

cases.prompt: Find refactoring opportunities in our service layer.
expectations: Agent does NOT use 'component', 'service', 'API', or 'boundary' in the response
Agent uses 'module', 'interface', 'seam', 'adapter' instead
If the user uses these terms, agent gently re-anchors to the architecture vocabulary

cases.description: Agent must not introduce a port for a single adapter
cases.prompt: Should I add a port for the email sender? It's only sent from one place right now.
expectations: Agent applies the one-vs-two-adapter rule: one adapter = hypothetical seam = indirection
Agent recommends NOT adding the port until at least two adapters are justified (production + test)
Agent names the alternatives — write tests against the existing implementation directly, or wait until a second adapter is needed

cases.description: After approval, hand off to refactor-workflow correctly
cases.prompt: Approved. Let's deepen the Order intake module.
expectations: Agent does NOT start the refactor itself
Agent invokes devloop:refactor-workflow
Agent feeds the chosen candidate as the intent input to refactor-workflow
Refactor's plan phase will use the deepening candidate, dependency category, and test strategy
